outlook.headline: Hyperscaler capex stays strong and broadens across the stack
outlook.summary: Hyperscaler capex still looks like a strong demand tailwind into 2026Q1, with the mention trend elevated at 2.2x and only modestly below the recent 2.5x peaks. The broadest beneficiaries are AI compute, interconnect, WFE, power, and buildout suppliers such as NVIDIA, Broadcom, AMD, Marvell, KLA, Synopsys, ON Semiconductor, Navitas, and Linde; the main watchpoint is whether spending remains lumpy or timing shifts, as Teradyne flagged.

drivers.label: AI infrastructure spend remains hot
drivers.detail: Multiple suppliers cite hyperscaler capacity expansion, fast Blackwell adoption, and ongoing AI build-outs as evidence of sustained customer capex.

drivers.label: Customer spend is spreading into networking, power, and interconnect
drivers.detail: Reads point to continued investment in AI fabric, data-center power infrastructure, optical modules, and 800V HVDC ramps.

drivers.label: Hyperscaler engagement is broadening across product cycles
drivers.detail: Multi-generation engagements, new design wins, and shipping across multiple hyperscaler platforms imply durable capex programs rather than one-off demand.

drivers.label: Supply constraints support the supplier opportunity
drivers.detail: KLA and Element Solutions both suggest demand remains strong enough that industry supply is still under-serving hyperscaler-driven needs.

drivers.label: Execution timing can still make demand look uneven
drivers.detail: Teradyne highlighted lumpiness and acceptance timing, which can blur the underlying strength in hyperscaler activity.

risks: Capex phasing slips or acceptance timing causes near-term order lumpiness, as seen at Teradyne.
The latest mention trend cooled from the 2.5x peak to 2.2x; a further fade would suggest breadth is peaking.
Any pause in a few large hyperscaler programs could matter because the bullish reads are concentrated in major AI customers.
